zh.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara是Gutier Pérez de Toledo和Mrsa Gutierre Perez的兒子，於1177年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多。他和Elvira Bernardo  Felipa至少有3個兒子。他去世在西班牙。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara是Gutier Pérez de Toledo和Mrsa Gutierre Perez的儿子，于1177年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多。他和Elvira Bernardo  Felipa至少有3个儿子。他去世在西班牙。

nameMeanings.name: Perez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish (Pérez): patronymic from the personal name Pedro, an equivalent of Peter . This surname is also found in France. Compare De Perez .
Breton (Pérez): variant of Pérès (see Peres ).

variants.name: Parez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Pérez (see Perez ).
Czech (Pařez): nickname from pařez ‘(tree) stump’.
In some cases possibly also French (Nord), Walloon, and Flemish: variant of Paré (see Pare ) or Paret .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: Ruiz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Luis, Miguel, Roberto, Pedro, Ramon, Jorge.
Spanish and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Ruy, a short form of Rodrigo .

variants.name: Ruisi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Santo, Angelo, Matteo, Carmela, Lorenzo, Pasquale, Vittorio.
Italian (Sicily): probably an Italianized form of Spanish   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Ruz
meanings: Spanish: variant of   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

variants.name: Ruis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Arturo, Carlos, Conception, Elba, Fernando, Jose, Josue, Juan, Jubenal, Lazaro, Lupe.
Spanish and Portuguese: variant of   Ruiz  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guzman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Pedro, Rafael, Ramon, Raul.
Spanish (Guzmán): of uncertain and disputed etymology, probably from an ancient Germanic personal name. Compare De Guzman .
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Gusman  .

variants.name: De Guzman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ernesto, Pedro, Manuel, Reynaldo, Ricardo, Jaime, Alberto, Alfredo, Edgardo, Francisco, Mario.
Spanish (De Guzmán): patronymic from Guzmán (see Guzman ), with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Gusman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Blanca, Jorge, Manuel, Salvador, Santiago, Adolfo, Alejandro, Alfonso, Alvino.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico; Gusmán): altered form of Spanish Guzmán (see Guzman ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): occupational name for a metalworker, from Yiddish gus ‘casting’ + man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: Gutierrez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Manuel, Carlos, Francisco, Jorge, Pedro, Raul, Miguel, Roberto.

Spanish (Gutiérrez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Gutierre, from a Visigothic personal name of uncertain form and meaning, perhaps a compound of the elements gunthi ‘battle’ + hairus ‘sword’.

variants.name: Gutierez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Ignacio, Manuel, Adalberto, Ana Maria, Angel, Carlota, Emilio, Exiquio, Felipe, Fermin.
Hispanic (Gutiérez): altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

variants.name: Gutirrez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

variants.name: Guterrez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

variants.name: Guiterrez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Arturo, Francisco, Alfredo, Jesus, Rafael, Raul, Carlos, Guadalupe, Jose Luis.
Hispanic (Guitérrez): altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

variants.name: Gutierres
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ramon, Jesus, Juan, Manuel, Alberto, Alfonzo, Alvaro, Ana, Andres, Antelmo, Catalina.
Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

variants.name: Guitierez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

variants.name: Guttierrez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Pablo, Raul, Reina, Adolfo, Beatriz, Carlos, Diego, Evangelina, Felicita, Humberto.
Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

variants.name: Guitierrez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Enrique, Jaime, Manuel, Miguel, Ofelia, Ricardo, Salvador, Andres, Argentina, Armando.
Hispanic (Guitiérrez): altered form of Spanish Gutiérrez (see Gutierrez ).

nameMeanings.name: Rodriquez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish (Rodríquez): variant of Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ). This surname is very rare in Spain.

variants.name: Rodriguez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Pedro, Raul, Miguel, Roberto, Ramon, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao, Ligia, Catarina, Paulo, Joaquim, Omero, Joao, Wenseslao, Adauto, Albeiro, Godofredo.

Spanish (Rodríguez) and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Rodrigo . Rodriguez (including in the sense 2 below) is the ninth most frequent surname in the US. Compare De Rodriguez and Rodriquez .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Iberian surname (see above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

nameMeanings.name: Senor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Andres, Blanca, Don Juan, Felipe, Valeria.
Spanish (Señor): nickname from the title señor‘mister’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Jorge, Luis, Pedro, Jesus, Roberto, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish: habitational name from a place called Lara de los Infantes in Burgos province. Compare   De Lara  .

variants.name: De Lara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Juan, Jose, Luis, Mauricio, Pedro, Alfonso, Ana, Jaime, Javier, Jorge, Merlinda.
Spanish: variant, with the preposition de ‘from’, of Lara .

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara was born in 1177, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain as the son of Gutier Pérez de Toledo and Mrsa Gutierre Perez. He had at least 3 sons with Elvira Bernardo  Felipa. He died in Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara est né en 1177, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, fils de Gutier Pérez de Toledo et Mrsa Gutierre Perez. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Elvira Bernardo  Felipa. Il est décédé à Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara wurde 1177 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien, als Sohn von Gutier Pérez de Toledo und Mrsa Gutierre Perez geboren. Er hatte mit Elvira Bernardo  Felipa mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb in Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara è nato il nel 1177 nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna come figlio di Gutier Pérez de Toledo e Mrsa Gutierre Perez. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Elvira Bernardo  Felipa. Morì nel Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Laraは1177年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドでGutier Pérez de ToledoとMrsa Gutierre Perezの息子として生まれた。彼はElvira Bernardo  Felipaとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼はスペインで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara(은)는 1177년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 Gutier Pérez de Toledo(와)과 Mrsa Gutierre Perez의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Elvira Bernardo  Felipa(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 스페인에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara nasceu em 1177, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha filho de Gutier Pérez de Toledo e Mrsa Gutierre Perez. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Elvira Bernardo  Felipa. Ele faleceu em Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara родился в 1177 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. Его родителей звали Gutier Pérez de Toledo и Mrsa Gutierre Perez. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Elvira Bernardo  Felipa. Он умер в местности Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara nació en 1177, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España como hijo de Gutier Pérez de Toledo y Mrsa Gutierre Perez. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Elvira Bernardo  Felipa. Murió en España.
